title: Crispy Seafood Scallion Pancake (Pajeon)
summary: An authentic, extra-crispy savory pancake packed with fresh scallions and a rich seafood combination.
tips: Always use ice-cold water! It slows down gluten development in the flour mix, resulting in a noticeably crispier, lighter crust.
Pat dry the seafood mix with paper towels thoroughly before adding it to prevent the pancake from getting soggy.

steps.title: Prep the Ingredients
steps.description: Thoroughly wash and completely dry the scallions. Cut them in half to fit your frying pan. Pat dry the seafood mix with paper towels to eliminate excess moisture.

steps.title: Mix the Batter
steps.description: In a mixing bowl, combine 1 cup of Korean frying mix, 1/2 cup of crispy frying mix, and 1.2 cups of ice-cold water. Whisk lightly just until the flour is incorporated.

steps.title: Assemble in the Pan
steps.description: Heat a generous amount of vegetable oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Arrange the scallions flat in the pan, ladle the batter evenly over them, then scatter the seafood and chilis on top.

steps.title: Bind and Flip
steps.description: Pour the lightly beaten egg across the top surface to bind the seafood. Cook until the bottom is golden-brown and crispy, then carefully flip, adding a bit more oil around the edges to crisp the other side.
